Despite my constant attempts to prompt action, my attorney seemed unresponsive, and important details were overlooked. It wasn't until I received a letter from the opposing counsel that really opened my eyes and highlighted the  lack of strategy of my previous firm. I knew right then  I needed to make an immediate change. I sought out a new attorney who would prioritize my interests and guide me through unfamiliar territory with competence and diligence.

Thankfully, I was referred to Ryan Young at OC Trial Group, and it's been a game-changer. Ryan's expertise is evident from the get-go, and his attentive approach immediately stood out during our initial consultation. He's not just knowledgeable; he's compassionate, informative, and, perhaps most importantly, he listens. In a field where responsiveness and empathy are often lacking, Ryan is a refreshing exception.

Now, with Ryan and The OC Trial Group on my side, I feel a sense of relief. Facing legal challenges can be daunting, and more importantly very stressful  but knowing I have a trustworthy advocate like Ryan makes all the difference. It's reassuring to have someone who truly has my back in the face of adversity.

Later, she became our lead business attorney revamping our NDA, Reseller, BAA, and various other business agreements as well as negotiating with customers.  She is very knowledgeable, detail-oriented, practical, realistic, and caring - when it came to helping us deal with difficult situations.

Ellie Vilendrer is hard working and cares deeply about helping parties efficiently resolve disputes. She asked thoughtful questions, actively listened, and helped us get to the root of the issue rather than staying stuck on surface-level disagreements. Ellie has helped me out with numerous issues and has earned my complete trust.

Ellie's approach is one of compassion, straightforwardness, professionalism and humanity. I contacted her about a SAG-AFTRA contract amidst our current global crisis and she got back to me in good time and with a strong sense of relatabillity. Would highly recommend.

I was provided a referral to Ellie Vilendrer when I needed some legal counsel regarding an IP matter. I could not have asked for better service. When I first called Ms. Vilendrer I expected to leave a voicemail or to schedule a future time for a consultation call. Instead, she answered and made the time right then to have a 45 minute consultation. She was attentive, quick thinking, encouraging, and transparent. I was so impressed that she immediately knew which questions to ask and which considerations were relevant to my case, without having any prior information going into the call. She clearly articulated what next steps I would need to take to organize my documents and offered a very reasonable rate. Ellie manages to simultaneously be professional and personable at all times. When I sent her all my materials, she reviewed everything and provided her analysis in lightning fast time - in about 24 hours!! Truly awesome. Even in that short turnaround time, she provided in depth analysis and recommendations. What really made her service incredible, though, is that she also provided genuine words of support and encouragement. That's going above and beyond in my opinion! The quality of her legal advice was proven by the outcome of my case. Following Ellie's instructions, I was able to effectively advocate for myself through email and in meetings with the university IP office. I have now resolved the dispute over my IP agreement and arrived at an alternative solution. I am so grateful to have found Ms. Vilendrer, and will certainly return in the future whenever I am in need of further legal services.

I couldn’t ask for a better experience working with Ellie. She went above and beyond and treated me like her own family through the process. She checked on me every single day, kept me updated, and answered all of my questions. She is very knowledgeable, sharp, and committed to her clients and I will absolutely recommend her to my friends and family. Thank you so much Ellie for your attention and time.

had just hired this firm for my work accident.  from the beggining there were red flags.  When i signed the retainer i had never spoken to the attorney only Dora.  A month and a half had passed and i had still not spoken to the attorney.  Upon reading the contract i saw that it says that Dora and another person will be authorized to file paperwork and deal with my case, big red flag, this means they do all the work and tge attorney just collects of is too busy to talk to his clients.  It had been a week after signing the retainer and i had not heard from anyone from the firm causing me to wonder if they were representing me.  I had to call a week later to find out they were in fact representing me, scary!  I asked Dora if the attorney calls to soeak to me at any point and introduce himself and she said they dont do that.  Dora even tried to tell me I dont have a third party lawsuit when i do.  She doesnt evem listen to people and worst of all i dont even think shes a parallegal.  I dont think this was the right attorney for me.  So glad i switched early rather than later as i never felt comfortable with them.  If you want a ghost attorney representing you hire them.  Or if  you want a person who doesnt specialize in law,  to deal with your case with never speaking to an attorney, youre in the rightplace .  i had asked to speak to the attorney.  Dora told me that she would schedule me to talk to the attorney following week and and nothing happened.

He didn’t just help with the cases, he actually got them removed so completely that even UBER and Checkr couldn’t see them anymore. Thanks to that, I was able to get a side job with Uber.

If you’re in a similar situation, here’s a helpful tip: when applying to Uber, use a brand new email instead of your old one. Older emails can sometimes be linked to your past records.

David is incredibly skilled at what he does. He kept me updated throughout the process — not only by email, but also through his easy-to-use website where I could track all the work being done.

I still have a few more cases, and I’ll definitely be going back to him later this year.

Highly recommend!

Thank you so much, David. The extra income from Uber has helped me more than I can say — and it’s already paid back the small, very affordable fee I paid upfront. Also, for those wondering: yes, he offers payment plans too!

reviews.text:
Updated Review – October 2025

Well, I never thought I’d be writing multiple reviews, especially not after retiring, but here I am again and for good reason. As a retired attorney with 32 years of experience, I don’t hand out praise lightly. But LS Carlson Law hasn’t just earned my trust, they’ve earned my ongoing gratitude. When I found myself stuck in what felt like a legal mess caused by an overly aggressive HOA, I knew I needed more than just decent representation. I interviewed three other firms. None came close. LS Carlson stood out right away. From that first phone call, I could tell they were different. Sharp, honest, strategic, and down to earth. No gimmicks. No sales pitch. Just real legal help from folks who clearly know what they're doing. These people don’t just take on HOA cases, they live and breathe this work. Their founder literally wrote the book BAD HOA and that kind of credibility really matters when you’re dealing with board members who think they’re untouchable. Once I hired them, they got to work right away. Calls and emails were returned quickly, updates came without me having to ask, and I always felt like I knew where things stood. They were tough when they needed to be and thoughtful in how they approached things. It was clear they were seasoned pros. Now here’s what brought me back to write again. Just a couple days ago, I was notified that two of the worst board members had stepped down. Gone. And if that wasn’t enough, I received a written apology from the board for all the “turmoil” they caused me and my family. That kind of outcome is rare. It felt good y’all. Real good. S Carlson Law didn’t just get me through a bad situation. They helped turn the whole thing around.
What truly sets them apart, even beyond the results, is their ethics. In over three decades of practicing law, I’ve rarely come across a firm that’s this principled. Everyone I spoke with treated me with respect and honesty. They reminded me why I went into law in the first place. And I want to applaud LS Carlson Law for their work in the homeowner empowerment space. It really shines through in both their podcast and their book. Those resources kept me informed and gave me a sense of control when everything else felt upside down. Just a great example of a firm going the extra mile for the public. Thanks to LS Carlson, this old Southern lawyer can finally sit back, enjoy the California sunshine, and not worry about a thing. No more legal stress. No more bad actors. Just peace of mind. f you're dealing with an HOA problem, do yourself a favor and give these folks a call. They’re the real deal.
reviews.ownerResponse: Myles, thank you for taking the time to share such a thoughtful and detailed review. Coming from a fellow attorney, your words carry special weight, and we’re truly honored by your trust and confidence. We’re thrilled that our team’s strategy, communication, and commitment made such an impact and helped bring real resolution to your situation. Your feedback means a great deal to everyone here at LS Carlson Law.

reviews.text:
I had the pleasure of working with Tate Casey to help my aunt resolve a sensitive issue with the Laguna Woods HOA. From the start, Tate stood out for his expertise, professionalism, and genuine warmth. HOA disputes can be emotionally charged and difficult to navigate, but Tate brought a sense of clarity and calm that made the process far less stressful for my aunt.

What impressed me most was how quickly Tate understood the unique cultural context of our situation. As Circassians, our community has a rich tradition of dance, music, and celebration. We’ve used the Laguna Woods clubhouses to hold dance practices and gatherings that are central to maintaining our identity and passing it down to younger generations. Tate not only grasped this immediately, but he also found the right tone and language to convey our story respectfully and persuasively during an HOA hearing.

Tate has a presence that naturally draws attention. When he speaks, people listen. His calm confidence, paired with a warm but authoritative voice, makes him a powerful advocate. He approached the case with sensitivity and empathy, which meant a lot to my aunt and our family. He didn’t just see a legal issue, he saw people behind it and made sure that was reflected in every step he took on our behalf.

Tate is really the whole package.
reviews.ownerResponse: Neva, thank you for sharing your experience with us. We're so pleased to hear that Tate was able to help you HOA dispute with such care and understanding. It's wonderful to know that he took the time to appreciate the cultural significance of your gatherings and represented your family's story with the respect it deserved. Your kind words about his advocacy and approach mean a great deal to us. We're grateful you chose LS Carlson Law and that we could make this challenging situation less stressful for your family.

reviews.text:
I reached out to this law firm, thoroughly explaining my HOA conflict over a $200 fine and seeking advice on whether it was worth pursuing legal action, as I felt the HOA’s actions were discriminatory. Initially, I was informed that I did have a case, so I scheduled a one-hour consultation for $450.

The consultation itself was informative and clarified the options available to me. However, I was ultimately told that resolving the issue would require a flat fee of $2,000 to address the HOA.

While I appreciated the clarity provided during the consultation, I believe the firm could have disclosed this fee structure upfront. Knowing the costs involved from the beginning would have allowed me to make a more informed decision about whether to proceed. As it stands, I feel that the initial process lacked transparency and added unnecessary frustration to an already emotional situation.
Mr Lehman listened to my concerns and provided the necessary information to move forward.

Outstanding Attorney – Case Won January, 2026

I cannot say enough good things about Attorney Ron Cordova and the way he handled my case. On January, 2026, he successfully won my case, and that outcome was the result of his experience, preparation, and dedication from start to finish.

From the very beginning, Mr. Cordova was honest, realistic, and highly knowledgeable about the law and how the system really works. He took the time to explain every step of the process, answered all my questions, and made sure I understood both the risks and the strategy. I never felt rushed or passed off to someone else. He personally handled my case, which made a huge difference.

What stood out the most was how confident and prepared he was in court. It was clear that the judge and opposing counsel respected him. His decades of experience showed in the way he argued the case, anticipated issues before they came up, and protected my rights at every turn. When it mattered most, he delivered exactly what he promised: “results.”

Facing a legal case is incredibly stressful, but having Ron Cordova as my attorney gave me peace of mind. He was professional, compassionate, and fiercely committed to winning. Thanks to him, I can now move forward with my life.

If you are looking for a criminal defense attorney who truly knows what he is doing and will fight for you, I strongly recommend Ron Cordova. He earned my trust and my gratitude.

Working with Ron Cordova was the best decision I could have made. Dealing with a misdemeanor is intimidating, but he kept me informed every step of the way and truly fought for me. He was incredibly responsive, always taking the time to answer my questions and address my concerns quickly. I felt like my case was a priority to him. Thanks to his hard work, my case resulted in the best possible resolution. If you want a lawyer who actually listens and delivers results, look no further.

When I found myself searching for criminal attorney’s in the vast sea of Southern California, I felt my concerns were being swept under the rug by most to say the least.  Ron was extremely professional, knowledgeable and always went the extra mile to support me in my case. I highly recommend him to anyone seeking legal assistance in Southern California

I can't recommend Mr. Cordova highly enough. When I first came to him, I was facing the worst moment of my life—serious criminal charges and a custody case, both hitting at the same time. My daughter was four years old, and we had been separated.

From the get-go, Mr. Cordova understood that reunification with my daughter had to come first. Getting her back required me to prove I was a fit parent by going through various programs and rehabilitation. But all that could only begin by demonstrating accountability for my actions, which meant outright admissions to various wrongdoings in the case—making the criminal case harder to defend. Most attorneys would have seen this as a conflict. Mr. Cordova saw it as a sequencing problem and helped me navigate it with patience and a steady hand.

For two years, we fought to reunify our family. And we did. My daughter is now six, and she's home with me.

Only after that battle was won could we finally turn our focus to the criminal matter. But unfortunately, and not by choice, I handed Mr. Cordova even more hurdles: immigration considerations that severely limited our options in the plea bargaining.

In the end, short of an outright dismissal—which just wasn't going to happen—Mr. Cordova delivered the best possible outcome. He got 3 out of 4 charges dismissed, and we pled guilty to one—the only charge that I could live with on the immigration side. He took a case that felt impossible at the start and turned it into something I can live with, build on, and move forward from.

Mr. Cordova was referred to me by my bail bond agent as "the best criminal defense attorney he knew of," but he turned out to be so much more. If you're facing a situation where everything feels stacked against you—criminal charges, family court, immigration consequences—Mr. Cordova is the attorney you want. He sees the whole chessboard, and I really don't think I could have gotten through this with anyone else.

reviews.text: I never thought that I would need to seek counsel from a divorce lawyer. I did not know the first thing about the legal system, the divorce process, or quite frankly, what questions I needed to ask. When I was recommend John Bledsoe as the best family law attorney in Orange County, naturally I looked him up online. I went online and found his website to be extremely helpful. He gives you access to his book “Split Decisions” which essentially gives you a guideline to the divorce process, and some very helpful advice. This information was exactly what I needed, and I received it free of charge with no strings attached. I immediately called the same day to schedule a consultation. I was able to make an appointment that same week. During the consultation, John took his time to get to know me and my current situation. I can tell that he genuinely cared and is very passionate about his job. He answered all of my questions and made me feel comfortable with the whole situation, so much so, that I was able to provide him with details of my marriage that would be difficult to share with someone you just met. At that moment, I felt that he would would do everything he could to ensure my children’s and my future. It was very reassuring. At the end of the consultation, he made it known that I could text or call him with any further questions or concerns. The next day, I texted him and he responded, and we were able to have another conversation to address my new concerns. He takes the time to walk you through the process and does it in a way that feels like he is family supporting you through this difficult time. That kind of care is rare! If there is anyone that I would want to take my case, it’s John! His experience and reputation speak for themselves, but once you get to speak to him and know him, you understand why he is the best divorce lawyer in Orange County!
reviews.ownerResponse: Catalina, thank you for sharing such a thoughtful review. It means a lot to hear how helpful the website and the “Split Decisions” book were as you started learning about the divorce process. John cares deeply about guiding people through difficult situations and taking the time to understand each client’s story, so it is wonderful to hear that you felt supported and comfortable during your consultation. Your kind words about his dedication to protecting your future and your children’s future mean a great deal.

reviews.text:
I am not typically someone who writes negative reviews, but this firm’s handling of my case was so deficient—and completely lacking in accountability—that I feel compelled to share my experience to help others make an informed decision.

John Bledsoe was originally referred to me by a client, which created an initial sense of trust and confidence that my custodial matter would be handled with care and urgency. When I contacted the firm, however, I was redirected by Katie to another attorney, Ronald. I was under the impression this would be a temporary consultation, but it became clear he was assigned to my case.

Most concerning was the handling of my Ex Parte filing. For those unfamiliar, an Ex Parte is an emergency request asking the court to intervene immediately. My situation warranted this action. Despite accepting a $7,500 retainer, the firm failed to identify itself as my legal representation on the filing, submitting it as if I were self-represented. Any reasonable practitioner understands that this distinction matters and can materially impact how the court views the request.

Additionally, I was never informed that opposing counsel could file a response before my Ex Parte was even submitted—information that would have been critical to my understanding of the process and potential outcomes.

To further compound the issue, the firm later filed a Request for Order (RFO) but failed to file the required proof of service. This omission resulted in my hearing being deferred by two months. In a custody matter, that delay is not merely inconvenient—it is extremely distressing. The firm attempted to place blame on my new counsel, yet I could find no written communication indicating that immediate action was required or that responsibility had been transferred in such a time-sensitive manner.

What makes this especially troubling is the firm’s own marketing. John Bledsoe’s website states:

“Personal Attention, Not Hand-Offs: Unlike larger firms, John Bledsoe personally handles your case from start to finish; you get the expert, not an associate.”

At no point did I have any correspondence—by phone, email, or meeting—with John Bledsoe. Instead, my case was passed among multiple individuals, including an associate attorney (Ronald), a graduate law clerk (Sarah), a paralegal (Monique), and the office manager (Debbie Parker).

To be fair, Debbie genuinely tried to be helpful and do the right thing, and Monique was consistently pleasant and professional in her communications. However, those positives do not outweigh the serious procedural failures, lack of transparency, and overall mismanagement of my case.

Based on my experience, I cannot ethically recommend this firm.
reviews.ownerResponse: Thank you for taking the time to share your experience. We understand how stressful and emotionally charged custody matters are, especially when emergency relief is involved, and we do not take feedback like this lightly. While we cannot discuss case specifics publicly, we do want to clarify that our firm followed applicable court rules and procedures in your matter, including notice and filing requirements, and there were actions by opposing counsel that were outside of our control. We also made efforts to preserve time sensitive evidence at your direction and communicated extensively, including outside normal business hours, to address urgent concerns. That said, we regret that our communication and process did not meet your expectations, and we are sorry for the frustration you experienced. We appreciate you acknowledging the professionalism of members of our team, and we wish you the best moving forward with your case.

The Bledsoe Firm

reviews.text:
Dear Mr. Branigan Robertson

I hope this message finds you well. My name is Jodi, and I wanted to reach out to express how grateful I am for the work you do. I’ve watched so many of your YouTube videos, and they’ve helped me more than I can ever fully express. In some of the most confusing and difficult moments of my case against Kaiser Permanente, your voice and your explanations were what helped me feel grounded, informed, and not so alone.

Even though you weren’t able to take my case, the knowledge and clarity you share so generously have guided me through a process that has often felt intimidating and overwhelming. During my deposition, opposing counsel actually laughed at me when I said I had learned so much from YouTube videos. What they didn’t understand is that your teachings have been a lifeline—giving me understanding, confidence, and the ability to advocate for myself in a system that hasn’t always treated me fairly.

I have a very important hearing coming up on December 16, 2025 and I would be truly honored if you would consider attending. Whether simply as an observer or as someone committed to fairness and transparency, your presence would mean more to me than you know. I completely understand how busy you must be, so please know there is no pressure—just a very heartfelt invitation.

December 16, 2025 @ 8:30am
Chatsworth Courthouse
9425 Penfield Ave.
Chatsworth, CA. 91311

Thank you for everything you do. You have made a real difference in my life, and I am deeply grateful.

Warmly,
Jodi Vieira

I'm very happy I chose Mr. Rubalcava to set up my trust. What I thought was going to be an overwhelming experience was just the opposite. Because he is so knowledgeable, he was able to explain clearly and in a way that was easy to understand. I appreciated his patience and professionalism.

Very disappointed in this attorney. Went to him 5 years ago for a will. He recommended. I also do a transfer of death deed. Five years later I get a letter from the Assessor’s office that the deed needed to be corrected as he had crossed a sentence out which is not allowed. Called this attorney. He said he was out of town and would call me in a few days. Never received a phone call even after I called and texted him several times. What kind of person would not return phone calls to fix a mistake he made. Had to hire a new attorney to rescind that deed. My new attorney told me a transfer of trust deed is ill advised. He also put a secondary beneficiary which is not the correct thing to do as they would own the property as tenants in common. I plan on filing a complaint against him.

UPDATE: Shortly after posting my one star review, I was contacted my this attorney. He apologized and stated that he had surgery and thought his office had contacted me. He refunded me the entire amount that I had to pay the new attorney.
reviews.ownerResponse:
We are happy that you were able to get things taken care of and appreciate your understanding.

For the sake of people reading the review, I think it is important to mention that we also recommended that you also create a trust instead of a will, and even drafted a trust for you.

Your preference at the time was only a will and given there was real estate involved, the next best option was to create the transfer on death deed to accompany the will.

The new attorney drafted a living trust for you, (at a significantly greater cost than the will), which we paid for entirely to show we care.

We wish you the best.

reviews.text:
“I am so glad you found him!!” said my 96 year old mother said after Mr Rubalcava created her trust.
David was kind, knowledgeable, professional and answered all of our questions using easy to understand language. He has also been invaluable for subsequent changes as well. Additionally, he charges a reasonable fee, especially considering the quality of his work.

After deciding to have our Trust done & contacting several attorneys, my husband & I found David to be by far the most responsive.    David explained everything clearly to us,  & continued to do so throughout the process of writing our Trust.  He was always patient, professional, & kind regarding our questions & requests for changes.  We highly recommend David Rubalcava to anyone who is looking for a knowledgeable Trust attorney.

Mr. Rubalcava went above and beyond to help our family. He was professional and extremely patient with our 87-year-old mother. He took his time and was very thorough as we kept throwing new issues at him and having him make changes to what he was already working on. I highly recommend his services

David was absolutely outstanding in assisting my father and me with setting up our Trust. His professionalism, attention to detail, and prompt response made the entire process seamless. David congenitally answered all our questions and we had had a lot.

If you’re looking for someone who is knowledgeable, reliable, and cares about his clients, David is the best:-)

After multiple attempts to reach out, there has been a lack of response to calls and texts. I'm uncertain if this may be related to the concerns surrounding the will he wrote for my father, which lacked clarity and thoroughness.

I am now forced to go through probate if I don’t want lose a house that my father left. Who has the time and money for probate? I’d select a better attorney- one that would highlight the importance of writing a clear and concise will and living trust.
reviews.ownerResponse:
Hello Stephanie,

I am so sorry to hear about this. We tried to reach out to you in February but have not heard back. Please contact us. There should not be a reason for the estate to go through probate as all properties were titled in the trust correctly. Please call us and we will see what needs to be done.

I have another lawyer that was used for another trust and I am constantly following up with them. It feels Never ending. With David he calls me with updates. In this time of pain in my life, I really needed my Dad and felt that I had no one to trust, as people seem to love to lie just to get your money. David gave me honest straight forward advice; just like my Dad used to do. I don’t think he realizes what a blessing he was in my life and how thankful I am to say that David is my lawyer.

We were very pleased with our experience with David. He is compassionate, communicates effectively, efficient and doesn’t make you feel rushed. David is very knowledgeable and professional, and we highly recommend him!
Great trial team !

When this firm wastes your time with their false advertisement of a “free consultation”… don’t forget to file a complaint with the State Bar of California who will be happy to go take this firm’s TIME from them while handling your complaint.

The “intake” level employee was rude, talked down to me as if she’s some superior human being, and then tried to make me out to be “disrespectful” because I’m “expressing frustration” after being jerked around and treated like trash, as if my Time and respect doesn’t matter, only their Time here matters, and only they deserve respectful treatment in life and in business… Arrogant, and typical with those of malicious intentions behind making money.  “Low hanging fruit” grabbers that quickly and disrespectfully throw away the others that THEY consider “trash” as they speed through leads they capture with a false claim of a “Free Consultation.”

THEN, another person calls me to pretend to be of help, not even introducing her Name or Position (totally unprofessional).  She avoids my concerns, sidelines and cowers at my direct questions about the “CONSULTATION” definition and meaning behind their “lead-capturing” pitch/promise on their website.  She talks over me EVERY single time, like a truly manipulative “I’m better than you” attitude would do.  Disrespectful, unprofessional, low-hanging fruit grabbing firm just treating me like “trash” that they have no use for.

So, be sure to file a compliant with the State Bar of California so they can now spend all that extra saved “time” dealing with the state bar complaints.   And, don’t forget to leave your relevant Rating and Reviews on all the websites out there so that YOU can help prevent this from happening to others.  It’s unnecessary harm on the public, and I hope my review here helps prevent YOU and others from wasting your Time and your Stress, Frustration (your Health) like I did.

If I would have seen a review like this, I would have never called in to be put through that treatment, as I’ve seen it before with other Attorneys and I finally know what all to look out for.

Here’s what to LOOK OUT FOR and what to expect:
- an “intake specialist” that is difficult to understand with a poor phone connection, communications, and attitude of arrogance.  “MOST” are OUTSOURCED from a foreign country where our privacy Laws likely don’t have jurisdiction, and now you’re giving YOUR personal information to an outsourced “intake specialist” if so.
- the attorney never gets on the phone and NOBODY “consults” on anything.  Only an attorney can “consult” but you’ll never get that respectful consultation before you MUST give up a lot of your Data, Contact info, Personal Info (often including your Address, Email, Phone, Income, Employer Name, and tons of other personal information.

It’s a Lead-Capture situation FIRST AND FOREMOST with your personal data being given.  It’s very unsettling, and then you don’t get the “free consultation” rather, they lie to you by repeatedly saying “the intake IS the free consultation”…

NO. NO. NO…

A “CONSULTATION” given, has nothing to do with a non-qualified person ONLY asking personal questions, and never “consulting” on it… because they legally CANNOT consult.

Then, they refuse to give you a consultation, and you never talk to an attorney. I hope the State Bar gets enough complaints to make a change to this misleading tactic going on with law firms.  FILE YOUR COMPLAINT with State Bar, and avoid these other law firms that are doing this, there are still law firms that don’t do this.

And, by the way… the person who called me back from Aegis Law Firm said… “we primarily focus on CLASS ACTION LAWSUITS”…

Right there, is exactly what I’m talking about… I never should have been wasting time with this law firm “intake” runaround.  THIS ONLY LEAVES ROOM for the CHANCE that I was going to be a “low hanging fruit” where they would possibly venture out of their typical area of focus, for easy-money.
reviews.ownerResponse: Thank you for taking the time to share this detailed feedback — we are sorry you had a negative experience and sincerely apologize for any disrespect or confusion you encountered. We take concerns about staff conduct and advertising seriously, will review this interaction with our intake team, and provide additional training as needed to ensure calls are professional and transparent. To clarify, our advertised "free consultation" begins with an intake to gather basic information at no charge so an attorney can evaluate the matter; when legal advice is appropriate an attorney will follow up directly. If you would like to escalate this matter, please contact our Client Relations Manager at clientrelations@aegislawfirm.com or (555) 123-4567.

reviews.text:
I'm eternally grateful to Ian because within a month or two, pro bono, he was able to get my daughter a settlement beyond my dreams.  She now has a Trust.   I never even imagined or thought that this was possible, I simply just wanted reimbursement from the insurance company.   Ian went above and beyond.   Thank you Ian and your wonderful team!

It was my fist car accident. They told me "no" and refused to deal with me anymore.  I didn't know an insurance company had the right to do that.   I was in complete dispair.   There was a 3 car pile up on the 5 and we were rear ended.  I just figured I'll let my insurance handle everything, after all a commercial vehicle ran into us, regardless I'll be covered.   My insurance informs me to deal with the at fault drivers insurance- Liberty Mutual.  I had my 3 kids with me during the accident.   Liberty Mutual immediately sent me a check for the car seat and the repair fees, along with a rental.  Wow this is great so far.  Liberty mutual has another department for injuries.  The guy told me just go get medical treatment and save all receipts.   After my oldest had to drop out of dance due to back injuries and she went through depression for not being able to dance and enjoy life as a teen,  she also gained weight from pain.    I too, experienced the same.  Sadly, I have an autistic daughter. Also in the car, who I care for mentally and physically.  After a year of treatment for my and my daughters, including endless restless nights and crying and not being able to be the mom that I was, Liberty Mutual denied all my medical claims for me and my children.   I was devastated.  I had used my credit cards to pay for all my medical bills, thinking I'd just be reimbursed.  I wasn't even asking for pain and suffering, just reimbursement.  They told me "NO!" We don't feel you or your daughters needed all that medical treatment. Because these days they make bumpers so safe, not a lot of damage showed on my Mercedes.  However the impact was there.  This is something to consider in your furrier car accidents.   Insurance companies are still relying on physical damage to estimate your injuries.  Today the automobile industry will guarantee you how the new and improved bumpers are made to withstand more pressure and are more flexible.  So we're screwed. Least I was.  I'm out a couple of grand and I feel it wasn't even my fault. I followed what Liberty Mutual had told me to do- and in the end they said "NOPE & Stop contacting us!"   There it is. The real system of going through the insurance agent yourself.

Ian Silverthorne is our savior.  He took my case pro bono because he was so upset with Liberty Mutual and what they did to me.  Within two months I had a huge settlement from Liberty Mutual.  Ian came through for me and my girls.  He got me out of debt and he managed to get my Autistic Daughter a Trust!!!!  This is more than I was asking for with Liberty Mutual.   I just wanted reimbursement and to love on with my life.   Ian goes above and beyond that.

reviews.text:
I cannot say enough great things about O'Connor Law Group. From start to finish, they handled my lemon law case with professionalism, expertise, and genuine care. Being a family-run firm, you can truly feel the personal attention and dedication they give to their clients.
My case had several complications, but their team never backed down. They brought in the best experts, stayed proactive, and were always on top of every detail. I was consistently kept informed and never felt left in the dark.
Thanks to their hard work and persistence, we won my lemon law case! If you’re looking for a law firm that knows what they’re doing and truly fights for you, I highly recommend O'Connor Law Group. They absolutely deliver results.

Absolutely recommend! Great experience, quick resolution in comparison to other people telling me about their stories with other firms. Very happy with my settlement. Everyone there is sharp and on their game, as you can tell by the numerous 5 star reviews.

O’Connor Law Group was by far the most professional and supportive law firm I’ve ever worked with. From start to finish, the entire team made a very stressful lemon law situation feel manageable and straightforward.

David Womack, my paralegal, was incredible and stayed with me every step of the way, always responsive and reassuring. My attorney, Matthew Goethals, was extremely professional, knowledgeable, and guided me through the entire process with ease, clearly explaining my options and offering sound advice throughout. Rebecca Goethals was also always available and made sure every loose end was handled and clearly communicated.

My 2022 Chevy Silverado was a lemon from the very beginning—headache after headache—but O’Connor Law Group made the entire process with GM so easy and stress-free.

I strongly recommend O’Connor Law Group to anyone dealing with lemon law issues. They truly go above and beyond for their clients.

We were referred to O'Connor Law Group for a truck we believed was a "lemon" and needed to go back to manufacturer.  They were extremely professional, prompt and got the job done for us.  Our truck was purchased back by Ford and from start to finish was very easy and seamless.  We highly recommend them!
Extremely pleased with this firm. Shawna and the whole team were attentive, responsive, and truly cared about ensuring I was taken care of. Highly would recommend.

Great experience! I had gone to a different lawyer first for my lemon car and they wanted to straightaway file a lawsuit and i was told that the timeframe for resolution would be 6-9 months at least.. all this while, i would have to drive the defective car.

I talked with David who advised me to first send a message straight to Kia asking for a resolution. He drafted the letter which i sent to Kia and in a week they responded that they will buy back my car for full money back. David guided me through the whole process and a month and a half later l, i surrendered the car and got all the money back.

Very happy with the straightforward resolution!

I want to thank the entire team: Matt, Rebecca, Kristin, David, and Julia for the amazing support they provided. From the very beginning, they made the whole process simple and stress-free. Their dedication, commitment, and teamwork truly showed in every detail. I’m very grateful for all the effort they put in, and I would definitely recommend their services to anyone looking for help. I received my settlement, and I am super happy.

I’m incredibly grateful to O'Connor Law Group, P.C. for their outstanding work in successfully settling my lawsuit. The entire team—Julia, Rebecca, and David—were professional, attentive, and highly effective throughout the entire process.

Their dedication, clear communication, and support made a challenging time much easier to navigate. I couldn’t have asked for a better legal team to represent me. Thank you again for delivering a result that exceeded my expectations. Highly recommend!

I had just applied to law schools when my lemon started acting up. I watched the case develop closely, as did my attorneys, and I could not have been more pleased with how timely and professional O’Connor Law Group, P.C. handled my case and secured my settlement. Thank you for making this process as efficient and friendly as possible and I definitely recommend your services to anyone.

Trust the process. They are all about process and it may take some time but they are dedicated and strategically focused on maximizing awards. Great attorneys and staff, especially attorney Elizabeth. Recommend strongly. Great job everyone. Thank you.

Super helpful.  I had a nightmarish experience with the manufacturer of my new luxury EV.  Vehicle was in the shop for three continuous months and they were unable to correct the problem.  David Womack and his team were super responsive and informative.   I hope I never have to go through this again, but if I do I know who I would call.

Simply a great smooth and professional experience, the team is amazing at O'Connor Law Group!
After the dealer kept my car they ghosted me and the rental place! (It was a nightmare!).
Matthew was quick and made sure by the next day the rental was agreed on by GM! Any bump I ran in to, the team was easy to reach and great at helping me solve it. At the end, when it came to wrapping everything up,  David and the team were on it!  No hiccups, nothing stressful and simply smooth!
I hope I don't ever have to file a Lemon Law again, but if I do I would use O'Connor Law Group again!

OLG was great!  The staff and the attorneys.  We were kept informed and updated during the litigation process.  Our questions were answered within 24 to 36 hours, and sometimes, within minutes of us sending an email.  We highly recommend OLG if you find yourself needing a "lemon-law" attorney.

I had a great experience working with the OLG team. Everyone is knowledgeable and friendly. There were a lot of things I was unsure about regarding CA Lemon Law but they were patient in explaining the process. I didn't have to do much, provided them everything I had and they took it and helped me win my case before my trial date. I'm happy with the results. Even the manager at the dealership said she'll save their contact for her personal reference because they were so great at what they do!

I wholeheartedly recommend O'Connor law group as they have been very helpful in handling my the situation with my manufacturer. Special shout out to David, who followed up through the process and always had nuggets of wisdom on how to tactfully navigate the latest hurdle. 5 stars.

That’s a trifecta in anybody’s book.

Every bit true.

To my surprise and relief, he said I did not need to hire him at my stage, and affirmed the course and role I was taking. He also expressed personal interest in hearing how the case developed afterwards and being available for questions and second-opinions at no charge.  This was one of the few professionals I spoke-with over this probate journey who had a humble, kind, and servant-character of going above and beyond.  Thank you Erik!

Extremely professional and very easy to get in touch with.  Paralegal was very helpful.  I would recommend this lawyer group.  Also cost was very reasonable.

He is a good and very efficient lawyer. He resolved my case in a few months.

I would highly recommend Dave if you are in an accident or have any other type of personal injury case. I was injured in an accident in February 2016 and my car was totaled. Dave took care of everything and my case settled in August 2017. Most PI cases take some time and won’t be settled overnight. Constant back and forth with insurance companies, the length of time it takes to reach a point of maximum medical improvement from your injuries, plus obstacles from the other party involved all play a role in the length of a case. So if you aren’t just “in it for the money” like most people are, be patient and justice will be served in the end by Dave Coffman.

Terrible personal injury attorney. I was under the impression that the very purpose of a lawyer is for them to fight to win your case on your behalf despite insurance companys trying to deny any fault in a relatively straightforward personal injury claim. NEVER before have I experienced someone giving up so easily and not making any effort to defend their client. I DO NOT recommend this attorney for any reason whatsoever, only concerned with his own interest, extremely passive aggressive on terms of defending his clients.
update: poor experience over the phone.

Jennifer is a strong and professional choice with a settlement focused practice. Through her guidance, I evolved from a place of fear to a place of empowerment (sometimes it was two step forwards, one step backwards, but hey, growth isn't linear). When I saw things through a specific lens she kept me in check and also went to bat for me when needed.

She navigated through tough scenarios with varied options, combative opposing counsel, many, many questions, and always kept my child's best interests a priority.

I would recommend Jennifer Owens to anyone who is in need of a talented, empathetic and intelligent attorney.

I am extremely thankful and fortunate to have Jennifer Owens as my counsel.  After initially meeting several lawyers, I did not feel that any of them truly were focused on my best interest and therefore did not feel comfortable with any of them representing me in one of the most crucial and critical moments of my life.  I am so grateful that a family friend referred me to Jennifer Owens.  After calling her, she set up a meeting with me in her office immediately and met with me for over an hour for a consultation.  She was so professional, knowledgeable,  genuine and ethical that I knew right away she had my best interest in mind.  I felt very comfortable and open to discuss my case with her.  Since then she has always been available to talk with me when I need her counsel, and most importantly she has always been honest and direct to the point with me.  During court proceedings, her presence is calming and she is amazing in her delivery and confidence.  Her writing skills are excellent and she always makes sure that all paperwork is accurate, correct, and that I fully understand their importance and purpose.  She provides me with excellent recommendations and specifically reviews the pros and cons with me so that I can comprehend this confusing and emotional process.  This allows me to make the best decisions that will impact the rest of my life.  I can say that Jennifer truly cares and she gives you all of her attention.  I would not have made it this far without her, and for that I am and will be forever grateful.  If you are feeling lost and confused and unsure on how to proceed, I strongly suggest that you give the Law Offices of Jennifer Owens a call for a consult, and meet Jennifer Owens yourself.

The Law Offices of Jennifer Owens has been such a great choice for myself. This is the THIRD law offices that I have used in my divorce. The first was horrible, the second not much better and now Jennifer Owens has been terrific. The number one reason I like how she works is that she tells you the truth. I feel she is truly trying to help me resolve my case, not just trying to rack up attorneys fees. She listens to my issues, offers possible suggestions, tells me when I'm being unreasonable, and we develop a plan that specifically works for my needs. I would recommend The Law Offices of Jennifer Owens to anyone going through the difficult task of getting a divorce. She will handle your situation with care and treat you as a friend, not a dollar sign.

You are probably reading this because you are trying to decide which lawyer to hire for your DUI. You go through all these profiles and see everyone is 5 stars or close to it. So who do you choose? I was in the same dilemma a year ago. I interviewed several including this office. I even interviewed a high powered lawyer thats on TV and Radio. He was great to talk to also. I spent a week thinking about who I wanted.

I want to say that I never did speak with Mr. Levin but I did speak with Mr. Darin King. I don't know their process of selecting your lawyer, but let me assure you, it doesn't matter. I decided to go with them because the initial team including Mr. King made me feel assured they will keep my best interests in mind. One of the main reasons I selected them was that Mr. King appears quite often in the courthouse my case was being heard. I do believe that lawyers build relationships with each other rather than being adversarial. So I wanted someone who would have relationships with the DAs and Judges where my case was being heard.

My ordeal took almost a year. The penalties I was facing with were far exceeding what I was expecting. In the end Mr. King worked through my case diligently and negotiated on my behalf. You have to be honest with yourself first. If you did drink and drive, then be ready to face the penalties. Your lawyer can't get you off on a crime you know you committed but what they can help you with is to guide you to move forward and possibly mitigate some of your penalties. Personally I think my case and penalties could have been far greater had it not been for Mr. King. I'm grateful he was by my side. I also want to say that Stephanie his paralegal was amazing to work with. She was very detailed and always open to communication. Phone, text, emails, she was easy to get a hold of.
reviews.ownerResponse: Thanks Roger. It was our pleasure!

On 06/18/2022 , my life changed. After I was released from the police station the following day I felt alone, embarrassed, remorseful, hopeless, empty, sad and clueless as to how I was going to face my new reality. That day I was taught a big lesson that I will never forget. As a kid my father told me that two of the most important dates in someone’s life are, when you are born and when you find out what you were born to do. My dream and goal in life was to be a public servant as a firefighter/medic; thus, I always tried my best to maintain a clean record and contribute to my community. I achieved my dream and served my community proud, but that dream was short lived. For the last two years my life has been on stand by and every time I hear a siren on the street my heart shatters. From the moment I first spoke with you Mr. Levin I knew I had chosen the right attorney with the right team; you and Jon and all your staff gave me hope during a time of uncertainty. I will never be able to thank you all enough for how hard you fought for me. Today, my life has changed again but in a different way. Thank you again for fighting for me so that I can get my life back as well as fighting for me dream to become a firefighter again, it is people like you that motivate me to be the best version of myself. I will write a review for you all and with my hand on my heart I thank you again.

After a grueling battle with my mold-related illness case, I reached out to Strongin Firm at a time when I had completely lost faith in lawyers.

My experience with my previous firm had been nothing short of frustrating—years wasted, progress stalled, and a lack of understanding when it came to the complexities of mold litigation. It was my first lawsuit, and I had made the unfortunate mistake of hiring the wrong firm initially.

Then came Kseniya and the team at Strongin Firm. From day one, they not only understood the intricacies of my case but also moved with the speed, precision, and expertise I had been desperately seeking. What my previous attorney failed to accomplish in 2-3 years, Kseniya resolved in just a few months.

If you're dealing with a mold-related case and need fierce, competent, and results-driven representation—Strongin Firm is the firm you need. They turned my case around, restored my confidence in the legal system, and delivered the results I had fought so long for.

I cannot recommend them highly enough!

reviews.text:
Divorce is like setting your house on fire and then arguing about who gets the smoke. It’s expensive, emotional, and suddenly everyone you’ve ever met becomes a legal expert.

That’s when I hired Jennifer Posey and Associates.

First thing Jennifer did? She protected me… from me. Which, frankly, was the most dangerous person in the room. She kept me from making emotional, chest-thumping, “I’ll-show-them” decisions that feel great for 12 minutes and cost you for 12 years.

She listened. Really listened. Then she gave me information — not hype, not fear, not drama — just the facts. Pros. Cons. Reality. She silenced the outside noise so I could actually think.

I came in with boundaries.

I had my Alamo — the things I was not willing to lose.
Then I had the outer wall — the things I’d fight for, but wasn’t willing to die on.

I wanted fair and equitable. No malice. No revenge tour. The kids’ mom was, is, and always will be their mom — and she deserved to be treated with respect. At the end of the day, I needed to sleep with peace in my heart.

Jennifer promised she’d protect me from myself, keep me from making stupid emotional decisions, be honest — even when it wasn’t convenient — and walk me through the pros and cons of every major choice.

She did exactly that.

Her staff? Friendly. Kind. Professional. They answered the phone or called back quickly. They followed through. It felt less like calling a law office and more like calling people who actually had your back.

Here’s the truth nobody wants to admit:
Divorce is ugly. It changes people. Attorneys are expensive. The system in California can make you question your faith in humanity. And the outside noise? Deafening.

You need a quarterback.

Jennifer Posey is that quarterback. Calm in the chaos. Strategic. Honest. And when you’re about to throw an emotional Hail Mary into triple coverage, she calls a timeout.

If you want drama, hire someone else.
If you want steady, smart, and fair — hire Jennifer Posey and Associates.

I walked away with what mattered, my integrity intact, and the ability to sleep at night.

That’s worth everything.

I am so impressed by this lawyers expertise.  Ms Posey and her firm handled my case with such professionalism and achieved an outstanding result for me...  I walk away from this divorce with my head held high knowing that I retained the best attorney in Mission Viejo California and all of Orange County.
She will fight for you and go that extra mile.
I am profoundly grateful for their services.
L Darlene Correll

I would recommend and refer the Family Law firm Posey & Franzi to any friend or family member.  Jennifer put together a highly qualified Team and it shows in their performance.  My divorce case was no walk in the park.  Jennifer and her associates handled my complicated case with great professionalism, strategy and responsibility.  I had a very complicated estate with assets, business shares and family trust issues to navigate.  Jennifer and her associates developed a strategy and direction, both offensively and defensively, that resulted in the best outcome I could have possibly asked for.

Divorce is an intimidating process, which can overwhelm someone with uncertainty. I wanted to make sure I wasn't taken advantage of, and that what was best for my daughter and my circumstances would be as important to my attorney as it was to me. I'm so grateful that I trusted Posey & Franzi to work with. From my initial consultation, I could tell that I was in good hands. They were extremely transparent with me about the process and set proper expectations, and more importantly, they fought for what was best for me as if they were personally invested too, which is something that I think is hard to find and the reason I will never take my business elsewhere. The whole staff is friendly and professional, and they made (and continue to make) the process less daunting. I would would recommend Posey & Franzi to anyone and everyone looking for quality representation.

Jennifer and her staff are the best of the best. She was able to get me out of a difficult situation with her expertise and grace. While I was in the midst of a divorce from my ex-husband who would not budge on any financial matters. I was stuck with an incompetent lawyer who would refuse to fight for what I wanted. My divorce had been going on for 2 years at this point. Nothing was coming to an end. On top of that, I was losing massive amounts of money and my spirit was exhausted. But as soon as I switched to Jennifer, things immediately started looking up. Her assertiveness and strong communication skills were effective in every interaction with my ex's lawyer. She always responded to my emails quickly, keeping me in the know. Whatever came to me, she helped me get through it. Jennifer is extremely experienced, so I would strongly recommend her to be your family attorney.

I needed legal counsel and representation for several ongoing custodial battles after I was legally separated and for my divorce.  Everyone at Posey & Associates is highly regarded.  Numerous acquaintances informed me (unsolicited) that I was VERY well represented.

I consistently found everyone at the firm to be fully informed and up to speed on my case(s).  They are organized, efficient and sharp.  They operate as a unified, cohesive team.

Several things stood out for me with Mr. Franzi:

* He read every word of documentation I sent him (lots) and he remembered every detail of it.

* He made it clear to me up front and throughout, what would be useful to the case and what would be a waste of my time and money... and his.

* Regardless of the court of morality and righteousness, he only focused on winnable strategies in a court of law.  Though he is a sympathetic and good listener, he never deviated from whatever was the strongest legal path to victory.

* I was always copied on draft documents (which were compellingly written) before they were submitted.  I felt fully informed throughout the entire process.

* Lastly, Mr. Franzi does not go in for bombastic histrionics.  He is respectful and gains rapport with opposing counsel, judges and court staff.  He is unassuming in his personality but focuses on making a solid legal case.

On legal points, he is unrelenting but I've seen him use rapport and mutual respect to leverage reciprocity and sympathy to his clients' advantage.  He prefers to let the facts and evidence be the megaphone.

I felt I was in very good hands.  I referred my mother to him as well who also hired him.  I highly recommend Mr. Franzi of Jennifer Posey & Associates with the greatest confidence.  He will do right by you.

I had a pretty tough case and had worked with a number of different attorneys over the course of 10 years.  Jen was the best by far and her staff was always supportive and informative which I definitely appreciated.  I will be using Jen moving forward for all of my family legal issues.

I have had quite a devastating month with fighting HARD for my son after my ex surprisingly served me and tried everything to get sole custody. I cant speak more highly of Jennifer Posey and her team. She helped me get exactly what I wanted within 18 hours of learning my case. It brings me to tears. Her professionalism, knowledge, demeanor, and love for her job showed through from the moment I met her. Best decision was to hire her.

Jennifer and her staff are top notch. Jennifer is nononse and knows how to get things done. Her staff is quick and responsive. Posey and associates is a smaller firm so you don't get pushed around from associate to associate. My proceedings took over 3 years and Jennifer was by my side for the entire process.

Jennifer, and really her whole staff, really were so helpful and professional during my legal proceedings.  They are a smaller firm who has the time and desire to really dig deep and get to know their clients' situation and really work towards getting you the desired outcome.   If you are looking for real personal consultation and representation, I would highly recommend the Posey firm.

I don't even know where to begin! Shopping for lawyers in the beginning of this massive life change was hard enough. Luckily there's still good law firms that put their clients FIRST, and I found them!! Hearing all the lawyer horror stories had me scared and very untrustworthy but Jenn and Joseph were amazing! They took care of and listened to every want, need, and concern I had. Needless to say my divorce went well and I came out successful and unscathed, but only from the hard work Jenn and Joe put into my case. I only put 5 stars because I couldn't find the 10 stars button. THANK YOU!!

reviews.ownerResponse: We never represented a Madonna Gionta.  We did represent her sister's husband - the outcome was excellent.  This is a retaliatory review.  You would be surprised how many clients came to us because they know someone who was on the other side, and regretted it.  Sweet victory.

reviews.text: Jennifer and her team were extremely responsive, supportive, & dialed-in to the OC court system.  They had an excellent strategic approach to my case & helped me to achieve a positive outcome!

I retained Jennifer after my former attorney had failed over an almost two-year period to reach resolution over the division of my federal pension benefits.  She immediately took charge of the case and set the stage for a very favorable outcome.  This case was complex, as it involved meeting certain requirements set by the federal Office of Personnel Management.  Jennifer used her expertise and experience to reach an equitable settlement with opposing counsel.  My 2017 and many years hereafter will be better because of the quality and thoroughness of her work on my behalf.

"If you can't beat em, join em!"
That's the best way to describe how I came to know Jennifer Posey and Associates.
Many years ago, I was working with a friend, who was going through a very contentious divorce, and asked him how it was going. He said everything was going great until my Ex hired a new Attorney. He said the  new attorney came into court and turned his ex's ship around and made his attorney look foolish in court. I asked him what her attorney's name was and he said
Jennifer Posey. The rest is history, I have been using Jen for my family court issues ever since. I have also referred several friends who have been ever so grateful for the referral.

I was referred to Mrs. Posey from a co-worker and I was immediately impressed with her demeanor and professionalism.  I knew right away that I was in good hands.  Mrs. Posey KNOWS the law and knows the ins and outs of the court system like no other.  She is edgy when needed but always compassionate.  She looked out for all of my needs while dealing with difficult legal and court proceedings.  I would certainly use her again in the future and I highly recommend her.

I went to Jennifer Posey after going to court with a different attorney and what a difference she made for me. Mrs. Posey is very professional and possesses the knowledge to take on any situation. Going through divorce is a stressful time and with Jennifer’s demeanor and confidence with the task at hand, she makes the situation more manageable with a lot less stress.  Mrs. Posey takes the time to learn your case inside and out and works hard to get what you need out of your personal case. She is a very fair and up front person that is not afraid to tell you truthfully how things are going to go in the court room. In my case, I was amazed at her ability and willingness to stand up to another attorney and fight for what was fair for my case. She told me exactly what to expect and that is what happened and more. Jennifer came recommend to me from not just one person in my profession, but 6 people. We all had very similar outcomes and I attribute that to the one thing we all had in common and that is Jennifer Posey. I highly recommend having a consultation with Jennifer before you make any decision in an Attorney. As I was told by 6 other people, you will not be sorry that you did!!  Thanks Jennifer for the work that you do, you are truly a difference maker!

If you need a family law attorney...Ms Posey is the BEST. Ms. Posey and her staff are as good as they come.  You feel from the beginning as if they are personally vested and are truly being taken care of.  She was amazing, level headed and extremely knowledgeable.  Ms. Posey has an unbelievable presence about her and was my strength in an emotionally charged situation when I felt depleted and unable to fight for myself.  She handles herself with grace and dignity and sticks to the legal facts.  The entire staff is wonderful about communication and keeping you updated every step of the way (even helping you through all the crazy legal jargon :) She literally was able to get me every single thing I was hoping for and I am walking away from one of the worst times of my life, completely satisfied and eternally grateful to both Ms. Posey and her wonderful staff.  If you want the best in family law, Jennifer Posey is it!!

My divorce in 2003 left me with having to pay lifetime alimony. When the economy crashed in 2008, my income crashed, as well, and I was not able to keep up with my payments. Consulting another atty, I was basically told that there was nothing I could do until I was able to get current with my payments. In 2014, a friend who had used Jennifer, with much success, told me to give her a call. Upon meeting her, I was at once impressed by her knowledge, her honesty, aggressiveness, and great personality. I was SO glad to have her on my side. She has been in the arena for many, many years and it was very evident when the court date arrived. She was miles above the atty on the other side. In fact, we never went in front of the judge. Jennifer was able to negotiate everything without ever stepping inside the court room. In the end, my alimony was terminated. I had to pay some arrears and a lump sum step-down payment, but much less than the other side was pushing for. I am very please to have been represented by Jennifer. I would refer her to anyone seeking representation by a true professional.

The legal process was overwhelming to me. I had a different family attorney before her, so I can say that it makes a huge difference if you have bad or good representation. After initially meeting with Jennifer, I felt like I was in good hands. She is very knowledgeable and explained to me my rights and best practices for my actions to get results. I cannot say enough things about she helped me through this process. She is direct and set reasonable expectations for me. Most of all, she was quick in directing opposing counsel to make a decision and not drag on with unnecessary proceedings. In the time at the court house, it was obvious she is very highly respected among her legal peers and presiding Judges. I always had confidence that my best interests were front and center in every negotiation. I will be a client of hers for life!!! (Or at least if I ever have to go through this process again.)

After having to make many court appearances because of review hearings and my ex constantly filing things, I retained Jennifer and was so thankful for her work she did on my case.  She asked me pertinent questions to get to know my case and is so knowledgeable about family law.  She finally got things ordered that were so helpful for my daughter and I that I couldn't seem to do on my own.  I have had other counsel in the past and they did not care to know the details of my case as much as Jennifer.

Jennifer Posey is representing me in my divorce proceedings and she has made a scary and stressful situation less frightening and manageable. She has kept me informed and has really fought for what is best for me and my kids. Her knowledge and experience in Family Law is vast and gives her the ability to really represent her clients to the best of her ability. I am very happy with her performance as my lawyer and I will continue to use her for any of my family law needs.

She is both wonderfully intellegent and has the ability to make one feel certain that the interest of both myself and my children are of the upmost importance. I feel as though I have nothing to worry about with the matter at hand and she has handled my case with both professionalism and personal attention to detail. I feel very lucky to have been able retain her and I would highly suggest Jennifer and her firm to anyone looking for assistance in family law.

I regret hiring Larry Bellomo. I hired him for divorce on July 7th 2025. My wife didn't get served until July 21st 2025. Apparently Larry can't do much without his paralegal who kept leaving. On July 14th my wife served me with a TRO Temporary Restraining Order. I had 3 weeks until the hearing on Aug 4th. Larry told me that he couldn't handle my TRO on that day and told me I would have to get a continuance and wait another 3 to 4 weeks, mind you all this time I am basically homeless. I fire him on Aug 4th. (He refused to provide me with my billable hours/accounting.) This is not taking care of the client!!! DO NOT HIRE THIS GUY. All he did was answer some text and file my dissolution of marriage without having anything else like financial record... and charged me $2000. RIP OFF. My new attorney had to get my accounting sent to them. BUYER BEWARE STAY AWAY!!

Thank you so much!

So we hired him to take our case to get grandparents rights and other issues .
2016 .  I  paid him in 3 payments . While in court my grandson mom had her own attorney which did all the talking, my attorney never said one word  to make a point and try to win the case , I was sitting next to him and told him “ say something “ but he stayed quiet. reviews.text: I do not have enough good things to say about this lawyer. I had a semi difficult case as I was no longer living in the state that my offense had taken place. He was courteous, very professional. Never made me feel like he would not win this for me (which i know is his job) however he has a confidence that i knew i wanted in front of the judge. After a few years of my case being postponed on several occasions (probably due to Covid and other things) my case was finally dismissed!!!! His costs were I think reasonable for what I was asking him to do and for me to never come to CA to meet him or go to court. He has a portal to communicate through and made sure I knew everything that was going on step by step. He made it such and easy process for me. So Richard--thank you! reviews.text: I came to Richard with a DUI and was currently on felony probation. The judge and court wanted to put me into jail for 120 days for probation violations. But Richard did his homework and discovered their was a new law that came out on Jan. 2021 that said I could only be on probation for 1 year tops and I had been on probation for over that time. So Richard used that to keep me out of jail and I am very grateful of that.
reviews.ownerResponse:
Dear William,

It was great to represent you in that case, especially considering that the DA was urging the judge to go along with the probation officer's recommendation to sentence you to 120 in the Orange County jail. I am glad that AB1950 - the new law - was a benefit to you and saved you from serving the jail sentence and got your probation violation case dismissed.

Enjoy the rest of the summer!

Richard Wagner
Orange County DUI Attorney
